piece.title: Shake the Disease (FormaComposition)
piece.notes: v5: addresses the 'doesn't breathe' problem from v4 by going beyond rest_probability tuning. Three concrete changes — (1) chorus is split into chorus_a (dense, motif-rhythm harmony, both counterpoint voices active) and chorus_b (thinned: harmony drops to free/sparse, the lower counterpoint voice drops to rhythm_density:sparse) so the ensemble texture itself arcs across the chorus instead of staying flat for 12 bars; (2) counterpoint voices are staggered rather than both running at medium density throughout — chorus_a keeps both voices active, chorus_b pulls the lower voice back; (3) a scripted 1-bar 'breath' section sits between chorus_a and chorus_b — no counterpoint at all, melody sparse + high rest_probability, harmony sustained not struck. This is a genuine ensemble pause, not a randomized one, and it repeats identically (exact_repeat) both times the chorus pair appears, same as the chorus halves themselves. chorus_final is left untouched — it's the deliberate full-intensity climax and stays dense by design; inserting a breath there would undercut the bridge's build into it.

intro.notes: Bass pulse only. Primary melody (plea motif) enters as a fragment. Counterpoint voices silent here — sparse texture only. Harmony stays sustained, no strumming yet.

verse_1.notes: Dm-C-F-Bb feel. Primary melody carries plea motif, lyrical. Counterpoint 1 (arpeggio character) interweaves above; counterpoint 2 (counterline character) below.

chorus_a.notes: First half of the chorus, dense and propulsive — harmony strikes the full plea-rhythm chord hits, both counterpoint voices active at medium density. This is the hook hitting full force before it's allowed to recede.

breath.notes: Scripted ensemble pause — deliberately one bar, not a probability roll. No counterpoint block at all (true silence in both counterpoint voices), melody mostly rests, bass holds a low pedal, harmony sustains quietly instead of striking. This is the actual 'breath' — everyone pulling back together, not random per-note gaps.

chorus_b.notes: Second half of the chorus, coming out of the breath. Harmony eases off the motif chord-strike pattern (free/sparse instead of motif) so it doesn't immediately refill the space just opened. Lower counterpoint voice drops to rhythm_density:sparse — staggered against chorus_a rather than both voices running flat-out for the whole 12 bars.

verse_2.notes: Same feel as verse 1, motif transform applied to the primary melody. Counterpoint 1 slightly pulled back for variation.

bridge.notes: Bb-C-Bb-Dm. Tension and release. Counterpoint 1 and 2 move in contrary motion, building toward the final chorus. Left untouched — the build should stay uninterrupted into chorus_final.

chorus_final.notes: Extended final chorus — 16 bars, intentionally NOT split or thinned. This is the peak — full intensity, peak interweave, no breath inserted.

outro.notes: Voices dissolve in reverse order. Bass pedal on D. Counterpoint 2 drops out first, then counterpoint 1. Harmony returns to sustain, mirroring the intro.
